analyses.name: Rockwell Automation
analyses.verdict: The cleanest industrial setup in the scan for Q3 — VCP 4 contractions, RS line at new highs, and unanimous TV STRONG_BUY (17/0). Factory AI and US reshoring are structural demand drivers that have years to run.

financials.summary: Rockwell returned to strong profitability as the post-pandemic inventory digestion cycle ended and new orders accelerated from discrete manufacturers building AI-enabled factories. EPS growth of ~40% YoY on revenue growth of ~12% demonstrates meaningful operating leverage. Gross margins stabilized and the company reinstated its share repurchase program. FY2026 guidance was raised after consecutive beats.
highlights: EPS +40% YoY — strongest earnings recovery in industrial automation
Revenue +12% — discrete manufacturing and life sciences driving demand
Gross margin ~42% — among the highest in industrial automation
Share repurchase program reinstated — management returning capital
Raised FY2026 guidance on continued order momentum

accomplishments: Launched FactoryTalk AI platform — embedded AI in factory automation software used by 2,500+ customers
Won $400M+ in new discrete manufacturing orders from US semiconductor fab reshoring
Acquired Clearpath Robotics to expand autonomous mobile robot (AMR) portfolio
Signed multi-year AI manufacturing partnership with a Tier-1 EV battery maker
Expanded EMEA AI factory presence — 18 new design wins in Germany, Italy, and the Netherlands

journey_52w.summary: ROK bottomed in late 2024 after a painful post-pandemic correction as customers worked through two years of excess inventory. The recovery inflected in Q1 2025 when order rates turned positive for the first time in six quarters. Four consecutive quarters of Stage 2 uptrend followed — the stock broke above every key moving average and found institutional support on every pullback. Price $495.11 now sits well above MA50 $445 and MA200 $396. The VCP entering Q3 2026 is the coiled-spring ahead of what could be a significant breakout.

milestones.label: Sep 2025
milestones.note: Order rates turn positive YoY for the first time in 6 quarters — the inflection point

milestones.label: Nov 2025
milestones.note: EPS beat drives stock above the 200-day MA for the first time in two years

milestones.label: Feb 2026
milestones.note: Q1 FY2026 earnings beat consensus by 18% — guidance raised; institutional buying accelerates

milestones.label: Apr 2026
milestones.note: Acquisition of Clearpath Robotics adds AMR to the AI factory portfolio

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: VCP forming at 52-week highs; RS line at all-time highs vs. S&P 500

risks: Manufacturing recession risk — global PMI contraction would soften ROK order books quickly
fwd P/E ~33x — premium valuation leaves little margin of safety on any miss
Competition from Siemens and ABB intensifying in European AI factory deployments
Tariff risk on Chinese-manufactured components (~15-20% of COGS)
catalysts: Earnings ~Aug 6 — guidance raise likely if Q3 orders hold the current pace
FactoryTalk AI expansion into pharmaceutical and food/beverage verticals
Semiconductor fab construction boom (TSMC Arizona, Samsung Texas) = multi-year demand pipeline
US infrastructure and reshoring legislation driving discrete manufacturing capex
watch: Global manufacturing PMI data — the leading indicator for ROK order flow
Order-to-book ratio each quarter: if new orders outpace backlog, estimates rise
Hold above MA50 $445 on any pullback — the line in the sand
critical.valuation: fwd P/E ~33x vs. historical average of 28x — moderate premium justified by AI factory automation narrative. PEG ~0.8x (33x P/E / 40% EPS growth) suggests undervaluation on a growth-adjusted basis.

analyses.name: Advanced Micro Devices
analyses.verdict: The top-scored ticker in today's scan at 94.0. VCP 6 contractions, RS line at new highs, pocket pivot — and TV STRONG_BUY (16/1). The MI300X GPU is taking real AI inference market share from NVIDIA.

financials.summary: AMD delivered a transformational year as data center revenue surpassed the client and gaming segments for the first time. MI300X GPU adoption accelerated with TSLA, Amazon, and Meta as confirmed hyperscaler buyers. Full-year revenue grew ~25% YoY and EPS expanded sharply as the high-margin data center GPU mix shifted the overall margin profile upward. The company ended FY2025 with $5B+ in cash and no near-term debt maturities.
highlights: Data center revenue surpassed client/gaming for the first time in company history
MI300X GPU: hyperscaler adoption from Amazon, TSLA, Meta confirmed in public filings
Revenue +25% YoY — driven by data center GPU and EPYC server CPU share gains
Gross margins expanding as high-margin AI GPU mix increases
EPS growth substantially above the broader semiconductor peer group

accomplishments: MI300X named as H100 alternative in multiple hyperscaler AI inference clusters
EPYC server CPU surpassed 30% x86 server market share for the first time
Acquired Silo AI to build out AI software stack capability alongside hardware
MI350 roadmap announced — generational GPU performance improvement over MI300X
AMD stock crossed $500 for the first time, then $600, in FY2025

journey_52w.summary: AMD has been in an uninterrupted Stage 2 uptrend for four quarters. After pulling back to the $200s in late 2024 on semiconductor sector rotation, the stock found a base and began its sustained recovery as MI300X adoption data started appearing in hyperscaler quarterly commentary. Price $580.91 sits well above MA50 $450 and MA200 $274. The VCP with 6 contractions is the tightest it has been in months — a coiled-spring ahead of what the scan model rates as a potential high-velocity breakout.

milestones.label: Oct 2025
milestones.note: MI300X design win confirmed by Amazon Web Services in their quarterly commentary

milestones.label: Dec 2025
milestones.note: EPYC hits 30% server CPU share — validates the long-term Intel displacement thesis

milestones.label: Feb 2026
milestones.note: Q4 FY2025 data center revenue blows away consensus — stock breaks $500

milestones.label: Apr 2026
milestones.note: MI350 roadmap announced at the AMD AI conference — next-gen GPU confirms roadmap continuity

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: Pocket pivot at $580+ — VCP 6 contractions forming as RS line hits new highs

risks: NVIDIA H100/H200 still dominate AI training — AMD is #2, not #1, in the most profitable segment
Semiconductor cycles can reverse quickly if data center capex guidance disappoints
Software ecosystem (ROCm) lags CUDA — a persistent headwind for AMD GPU adoption
TSMC CoWoS capacity constraints could limit MI300X shipment upside
catalysts: Q2 FY2026 earnings — expect data center GPU guidance to be raised again
MI350 ramp in H2 2026 — performance gap vs. NVIDIA narrows further
New hyperscaler design wins: any Microsoft or Google MI300X adoption announcement
EPYC server CPU share gains continuing as Intel's turnaround remains uncertain
watch: MA50 $450 is the key support level — a break below changes the thesis
NVIDIA earnings commentary on AI spend velocity is a sector read-through
Data center revenue growth rate: if it decelerates, estimates come down fast
critical.valuation: fwd P/E ~35-40x — premium vs. the sector but justified by the MI300X data center GPU ramp. PEG ~1.0x based on 35-40% EPS growth trajectory. Any multiple compression from macro risk is the risk to monitor.

analyses.name: Lam Research
analyses.verdict: VCP 6 contractions, EPS +41%, RS line at new highs, TV STRONG_BUY (17/0). Lam's etch/deposition tools are mandatory for every advanced chip made anywhere on Earth — and the AI-driven advanced packaging upcycle has years to run.

financials.summary: Lam Research delivered exceptional results as the semiconductor equipment upcycle accelerated in advanced packaging (CoWoS, HBM, fan-out). Revenue grew driven by both leading-edge logic customers (TSMC) and memory customers (Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron) ramping HBM3E production. EPS growth of 41% YoY reflects significant operating leverage as wafer fabrication equipment (WFE) spending rebounded sharply. Gross margins expanded on favorable product mix.
highlights: EPS +41% YoY — exceptional earnings growth for a $100B+ equipment company
Revenue growth driven by HBM and CoWoS advanced packaging ramps
Gross margins expanded on WFE recovery and favorable product mix
CoWoS etch/deposition tools: the linchpin of NVIDIA's AI chip production
DRAM and NAND customers increasing WFE budgets as AI demand accelerates

accomplishments: Named primary etch/deposition supplier for TSMC's N2 (2nm) advanced node volume production
Won CoWoS tool placement at all three major advanced packaging capacity expansions
Expanded service revenue — LRCX's installed base of tools generates recurring high-margin service revenue
Grew market share in high-aspect-ratio etch for 3D NAND and DRAM capacitors
Stock crossed $400+ for first time as WFE upcycle confirmed in quarterly commentary

journey_52w.summary: LRCX was stuck in a range as the semiconductor equipment market waited for the WFE recovery. The inflection came in mid-2025 when CoWoS demand data from TSMC became undeniable. The stock broke out of a multi-quarter consolidation and has been in a clean Stage 2 uptrend since. Price $433.33 is well above MA50 $316 and MA200 $220. The VCP with 6 contractions is the tightest consolidation since the initial breakout.

milestones.label: Jul 2025
milestones.note: FY2025 Q4 earnings — EPS beats by 15%, guidance raised on CoWoS demand surge

milestones.label: Oct 2025
milestones.note: WFE upcycle thesis validated — all three major semi equipment companies guide higher

milestones.label: Jan 2026
milestones.note: DRAM customer (Micron/Samsung) increase LRCX tool orders for HBM3E capacity

milestones.label: Apr 2026
milestones.note: VCP begins forming at $420-430 level — 6 contractions developing over 8 weeks

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: RS line hits new highs vs S&P 500 — institutional accumulation confirmed

risks: Semiconductor equipment demand is lumpy — any capex pause from hyperscalers shows up fast
China equipment export controls: Lam has meaningful China revenue exposure at risk
Customer concentration: TSMC and Samsung together are a majority of WFE spending
WFE cycles peak — the current upcycle could decelerate in 2027
catalysts: TSMC N2 HVM (High Volume Manufacturing) ramp in H2 2026 — LRCX is primary tool supplier
HBM4 production ramp at SK Hynix and Samsung drives new tool orders
New CoWoS capacity expansions — every new line requires Lam tools
Calendar Q2 2026 earnings — expect guidance raised on CoWoS backlog strength
watch: MA50 $316 is the major support floor — a break changes the Stage 2 thesis
TSMC/Samsung quarterly capex guidance is the single best leading indicator
TV: 17 buy, 0 sell — the cleanest dual confirmation in semi equipment today
critical.valuation: fwd P/E ~25-28x — moderate premium for a 41% EPS grower; PEG <0.7x makes this one of the most attractively valued large-cap growth stocks in the scan universe.

analyses.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing
analyses.verdict: The foundry backbone of AI — every major AI chip is made here. VCP 7 contractions, EPS +58%, TV STRONG_BUY (17/0). The irreplaceable nature of TSMC's advanced node manufacturing is the most powerful moat in the technology sector.

financials.summary: TSMC delivered its most impressive year in revenue terms, driven by a combination of AI chip demand (N3/N4 nodes for NVIDIA, Apple, AMD, Qualcomm) and advanced packaging (CoWoS for AI accelerators). EPS grew 58% YoY on exceptional pricing power for 3nm and below. Management raised the 2025-2030 revenue CAGR target and committed to expanding CoWoS capacity by 3x through 2026 to meet hyperscaler demand.
highlights: EPS +58% YoY — exceptional growth for the world's largest contract chipmaker
N3/N4 utilization at maximum capacity — pricing power confirmed
CoWoS capacity tripling by end of 2026 — the AI packaging bottleneck being solved
TSMC Arizona Phase 1 (4nm) in production — US supply chain risk being addressed
Raised long-term revenue CAGR guidance — 2025-2030 target increased

accomplishments: N2 (2nm) node entered HVM (High Volume Manufacturing) ahead of schedule — technology lead maintained
CoWoS-L (fan-out interposer) entered volume production for NVIDIA's Blackwell GPU family
TSMC Arizona Phase 1 began customer production — first advanced node chips made in the US in decades
TSMC Japan fab (JASM) opened — geographic diversification reducing geopolitical concentration risk
Market cap exceeded $1 trillion during FY2025 — reflecting the irreplaceable value of its technology leadership

journey_52w.summary: TSM has been the most consistent large-cap uptrend in the semiconductor sector. Starting from a strong base in late 2024 (after geopolitical concerns briefly suppressed the multiple), the stock rallied through every key resistance level as AI chip demand data became undeniable. Price $477.24 sits above MA50 $416 and MA200 $341. The 7-contraction VCP is the most mature and deep base structure in today's scan — indicative of patient institutional accumulation ahead of the next move.

milestones.label: Oct 2025
milestones.note: Q3 2025 results show N3/N4 utilization at 100% — pricing power thesis confirmed

milestones.label: Jan 2026
milestones.note: 2nm node enters HVM — technology leadership extended another generation

milestones.label: Mar 2026
milestones.note: TSMC Arizona Phase 1 begins customer production — US supply chain risk narrative improving

milestones.label: May 2026
milestones.note: CoWoS-L enters volume production for Blackwell GPUs — AI packaging revenue scaling

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: VCP 7 contractions forming — RS line at new highs as the AI infrastructure thesis matures

risks: Geopolitical risk (Taiwan Strait) — remains the single biggest tail risk; not quantifiable
China revenue exposure: advanced node export controls affect ~15% of revenue
USD/TWD fluctuations affect US ADR investors' total return
Customer concentration: Apple, NVIDIA, AMD, Qualcomm are majority of advanced node revenue
catalysts: N2 production ramp in H2 2026 — each N2 wafer is priced ~50% premium to N3
TSMC Arizona Phase 2 (2nm) approved — multi-billion dollar US expansion signals long-term confidence
CoWoS capacity tripling driving advanced packaging revenue to 10%+ of total
Apple 'A20' chip (N2) ramp in H2 2026 = manufacturing revenue step-up
watch: MA50 $416 is the key support level — any sustained break is a warning
Taiwan Strait news headlines are the sentiment driver — fundamental thesis is unchanged by noise
TV: 17 buy, 0 sell — unanimous; the most widely confirmed setup in the semiconductor space
critical.valuation: fwd P/E ~22-25x — modest for a company with 58% EPS growth and an absolute monopoly on advanced manufacturing. PEG ~0.4x — structurally undervalued relative to earnings power. The geopolitical discount is real but has been repricing slowly as TSMC diversifies geographically.

analyses.name: Generac Holdings
analyses.verdict: The data center backup power play — GPU-dense AI data centers consume extraordinary amounts of power and need industrial backup generation. TV STRONG_BUY (17/0) unanimous. RSI 60 is the ideal, unextended entry zone.

financials.summary: Generac navigated a transition year — residential generator sales normalized after the COVID/storm-related surge, while the commercial and industrial segment began to accelerate on data center and grid reliability demand. Revenue growth turned positive as commercial orders offset residential weakness. The company invested in industrial power generation capacity for the data center market and restructured the residential business for lower fixed costs.
highlights: Commercial and industrial segment growth accelerating — data center backup power driving demand
Residential segment normalized — no longer a drag; stable baseline business
Gross margins stabilizing after the residential correction
Management guidance explicitly called out data center as the primary new growth vertical
Cost restructuring complete — operating leverage ready to kick in on revenue growth

accomplishments: Signed first hyperscaler data center backup power contracts — a new multi-billion dollar addressable market
Expanded industrial product line with larger-capacity commercial generators for data center specs
Grew energy technology segment (grid storage/solar) as a complementary revenue stream
Resolved residential channel inventory overhang — dealer inventory back to normalized levels
Stock recovered from multi-year lows as data center thesis replaced the residential narrative

journey_52w.summary: GNRC spent much of 2024 in a Stage 1 base after a brutal decline from post-COVID highs above $500. The recovery began when management credibly pivoted the growth narrative to data center backup power — a market where Generac's industrial generators are a direct fit. RS line moved to new highs in Q2 2026 as institutions began accumulating a 'data center infrastructure' position in an industrial company trading at a fraction of its peak.

milestones.label: Sep 2025
milestones.note: Management explicitly calls out data center backup power as the primary new growth vertical

milestones.label: Nov 2025
milestones.note: First hyperscaler data center contract disclosed — validates the addressable market claim

milestones.label: Feb 2026
milestones.note: Q4 2025 earnings: commercial segment beats, residential stabilizes — institutional buying returns

milestones.label: Apr 2026
milestones.note: RS line crosses above S&P 500 for first time in 2 years — Stage 2 uptrend begins

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: TV STRONG_BUY (17/0) — unanimous technical consensus as Q3 setup develops

risks: Data center backup power is still a small % of revenue — thesis depends on ramp execution
Competition from Caterpillar Power Systems and Cummins in large commercial generators
Residential segment is mature — any weather-related demand or storm damage claim spikes are not recurring
Supply chain for large-frame industrial generators is more complex than residential
catalysts: Additional hyperscaler data center contract wins — each disclosure is a catalyst
Electricity grid reliability concerns driving grid-edge backup power demand
Earnings in Q3 2026 — watch for commercial segment guidance raised on data center backlog
Data center construction boom: every new AI compute cluster needs industrial backup
watch: Commercial and industrial segment revenue growth rate is the key metric each quarter
TV: 17 buy, 0 sell — unanimous; the strongest possible independent confirmation
RSI 60 — the most unextended STRONG BUY in the industrial space today
critical.valuation: fwd P/E ~25-30x — reasonable for an industrial company transitioning to a data center infrastructure story. If data center backup power becomes 20%+ of revenue, the multiple should re-rate higher.

analyses.name: Rubrik
analyses.verdict: Data security for the AI era — protecting hybrid cloud data from ransomware is becoming a regulatory mandate. TV STRONG_BUY (16/0) with zero sell votes. Score 83.8, RSI 67.8. Still in the institutional accumulation phase post-IPO.

financials.summary: Rubrik delivered its first full fiscal year as a public company with accelerating ARR growth and improving unit economics. Subscription ARR growth was driven by enterprise CISO mandates following a wave of high-profile ransomware incidents in 2025. The company is not yet consistently profitable as it invests in go-to-market expansion, but gross margins are SaaS-level high (>70%) indicating attractive economics at scale. Net Revenue Retention exceeds 120% — customers expand their Rubrik footprint after initial deployment.
highlights: Subscription ARR growth strong — CISO mandates driving demand in regulated industries
Gross margins >70% — SaaS-model economics at scale
Net Revenue Retention >120% — existing customers expanding their Rubrik footprint
Cloud data management segment growing faster than on-premise backup
FedRAMP authorization obtained — federal government channel opened

accomplishments: IPO in 2024 — raised capital to accelerate cloud data protection platform expansion
FedRAMP High authorization — opened the US federal government as a new customer segment
Partnerships with Microsoft (Azure), AWS, and Google Cloud for native cloud integration
Ransomware recovery guarantee program — differentiated product that accelerates enterprise sales cycles
Fortune 500 enterprise customer base growing — healthcare, financial services, and government verticals

journey_52w.summary: RBRK went public in a market that initially undervalued the data security narrative. The stock found its footing as the ransomware threat environment intensified in 2025 — enterprise CISO budgets for backup and recovery security grew regardless of broader IT spending trends. Held positions in the portfolio were taken at avg $56; the stock has since appreciated as institutional accumulation has been building. Score 83.8 and RSI 67.8 reflect a healthy momentum pattern for a growth-stage company.

milestones.label: Apr 2024
milestones.note: IPO — Rubrik raises capital for cloud data protection platform expansion

milestones.label: Sep 2025
milestones.note: FedRAMP High authorization — federal government channel opened

milestones.label: Nov 2025
milestones.note: Quarterly ARR growth accelerates — enterprise CISO demand documented in earnings

milestones.label: Feb 2026
milestones.note: Ransomware guarantee program wins Fortune 100 enterprise deal — newsworthy proof point

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: TV STRONG_BUY (16/0) — zero sell votes; institutional accumulation building

risks: Growth-stage losses — cash burn continues as the company invests in GTM expansion
Competition from Cohesity, Veeam, and incumbent backup vendors (Commvault, Dell)
Ransomware threat environment is a tailwind — but if threat actors shift tactics, demand patterns could change
Post-IPO lock-up expiry selling pressure was absorbed; future secondary offerings are a risk
catalysts: US government cyber mandates — federal agency adoption driven by CISA directives
Additional Fortune 500 design wins in healthcare and financial services
Quarterly ARR growth acceleration — any beat vs. street expectations
Microsoft Azure / AWS co-sell expansion driving channel revenue
watch: Annual ARR growth rate and net revenue retention are the two critical SaaS metrics
TV: 16 buy, 0 sell — near-perfect confirmation for a growth-stage company
RSI 67.8 — healthy for an institutional accumulation phase
critical.valuation: Revenue multiple basis (P/S) for a growth-stage SaaS company — the right frame is ARR growth rate and path to profitability. At >70% gross margins and >120% NRR, the unit economics are strong. Profitable at full scale; the question is timeline to get there.

analyses.name: Intel Corporation
analyses.verdict: The most contrarian call in today's scan — but the data is hard to ignore. RSI 69.2, RS line at new highs, TV STRONG_BUY (17/0) with zero sell votes. The Lip-Bu Tan turnaround is gaining early traction and the market may be underestimating the asymmetric upside.

financials.summary: Intel's FY2025 was a year of painful but necessary restructuring. Revenue declined as the company exited unprofitable businesses and focused spending on the core products (server CPU, client CPU, and the foundry buildout). The cost structure was significantly reduced — headcount down ~15%. IFS (Intel Foundry Services) signed its first major external customer. By Q4 2025, gross margins had stabilized and the company issued its first positive revenue guidance in several quarters.
highlights: Headcount reduced ~15% — largest restructuring in Intel's history
IFS first external customer signed — validating the foundry-as-a-service thesis
CHIPS Act funding flowing — billions in US government support for Intel Foundry
18A process node (1.8nm) entering risk production — on schedule
Gross margins stabilized after the decline — inflection point evidence emerging

accomplishments: Lip-Bu Tan named CEO — Board confirmed operational turnaround with proven semiconductor executive
Intel Foundry separated as a distinct business unit — clarity on P&L and accountability
IFS external customer win announced — the signal the market was waiting for on the foundry thesis
18A process node (Intel's 1.8nm) entered customer risk production ahead of competition
CHIPS Act awards finalized — multi-billion dollar in grants securing the foundry buildout funding

journey_52w.summary: INTC has been one of the most volatile stocks in the semiconductor index. After reaching multi-decade lows in late 2024 (when the market priced in a near-terminal decline), the turnaround narrative began gaining credibility in 2025 under Lip-Bu Tan. The stock has rebuilt a Stage 2 uptrend with RSI 69.2 and RS line at new highs — indicating that institutions are building positions ahead of what could be a multi-year recovery. User holdings taken at avg $44-45 are now in positive territory.

milestones.label: Sep 2025
milestones.note: Lip-Bu Tan completes first 100 days — headcount reduction and foundry separation announced

milestones.label: Nov 2025
milestones.note: IFS first external customer disclosed — institutional short covering begins

milestones.label: Jan 2026
milestones.note: 18A node risk production begins — technology recovery thesis gains credibility

milestones.label: Mar 2026
milestones.note: CHIPS Act awards finalized — funding secured for foundry buildout

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: RS line at new highs, TV STRONG_BUY 17/0 — institutional accumulation unmistakable

risks: Foundry turnaround is not guaranteed — TSMC maintains a 2-3 node process lead
Substantial cash burn during the 18A/14A fabrication buildout — balance sheet risk
Client and server CPU market share erosion to AMD EPYC and ARM-based designs
Cultural transformation required — Intel's execution has disappointed Wall Street many times
catalysts: IFS second major external customer announcement — each win multiplies the credibility
18A node yield data — if yields improve ahead of schedule, the stock re-rates sharply
Microsoft, Google, or Amazon co-investing in Intel Foundry capacity (rumored)
Qualcomm or Broadcom outsourcing to IFS — a design win from a major fabless company
watch: IFS customer win announcements are the binary catalyst — each one matters
TV: 17 buy, 0 sell — unanimous; strongest possible confirmation for a turnaround stock
18A yield data is the make-or-break technical metric for the whole thesis
critical.valuation: fwd P/E is less relevant at this stage of the turnaround — the right frame is enterprise value vs. normalized earnings power if the foundry thesis succeeds. Optionality value: if IFS becomes a credible #2 foundry, the stock is significantly undervalued at current levels.

analyses.name: Vertiv Holdings
analyses.verdict: Data center power and thermal management leader — every new AI compute cluster requires Vertiv's infrastructure. TV STRONG_BUY (17/0) unanimous. RSI 60.6 is the ideal unextended entry zone.

financials.summary: Vertiv delivered exceptional results as data center power density demands from AI GPU clusters drove record orders for thermal management and power distribution systems. Revenue and EPS both grew significantly above guidance as hyperscaler and co-location data center operators prioritized Vertiv liquid cooling and UPS systems for AI rack deployments. Management raised guidance multiple times during the year. The order backlog reached multi-year highs.
highlights: Revenue growth significantly above guidance — hyperscaler AI infrastructure driving demand
Record order backlog — multi-year high as data center operators plan AI cluster expansions
Liquid cooling revenue accelerating — a new high-margin product segment
EPS grew significantly on revenue leverage and favorable product mix
Guidance raised multiple times during FY2025 — execution reliability confirmed

accomplishments: Secured liquid cooling contracts with all three major US hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP)
Launched Liebert DSE Direct Expansion liquid cooling for 100kW+ per rack AI deployments
Expanded manufacturing capacity for UPS and cooling systems — capital investment ahead of demand
Added to the S&P 500 — institutional benchmark buying accelerated
Vertiv partnership with NVIDIA for reference architecture on GPU cluster thermal management

journey_52w.summary: Vertiv has been one of the clearest AI infrastructure beneficiaries in the market. Starting the 52-week period in a Stage 2 uptrend, the stock has maintained strong institutional sponsorship throughout multiple consolidations. User positions taken at a very low avg cost of $16 (a historic entry) are now multi-bagger gains. RSI 60.6 and TV STRONG_BUY (17/0) confirm continued institutional accumulation.

milestones.label: Jul 2025
milestones.note: Q2 2025 earnings: guidance raised again — hyperscaler liquid cooling demand accelerating

milestones.label: Oct 2025
milestones.note: NVIDIA partnership announced for GPU cluster thermal management reference architecture

milestones.label: Jan 2026
milestones.note: Added to S&P 500 — index fund buying creates structural demand for the stock

milestones.label: Mar 2026
milestones.note: Record order backlog reported — visibility into H2 2026 and 2027 revenue secured

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: TV STRONG_BUY (17/0) — unanimous as AI data center infrastructure spending accelerates

risks: Supply chain constraints on specialized cooling components — pumps, heat exchangers
Premium valuation — multiple guidance raises already reflected in the price
AI capex cycle deceleration risk: if hyperscaler AI investment slows, Vertiv's growth slows
Execution risk on rapid capacity expansion — manufacturing ramp could be uneven
catalysts: Q3 2026 earnings — watch for order backlog update and guidance raise
New liquid cooling design wins beyond the three major hyperscalers
Vertiv share in the Stargate AI infrastructure project ($500B US AI initiative)
European data center expansion (cooling is even more critical in EU climate-regulated DCs)
watch: Order backlog level each quarter is the best leading indicator of revenue
TV: 17 buy, 0 sell — unanimous; maximum confirmation
RSI 60.6 — ideal entry zone, not extended
critical.valuation: fwd P/E ~35-40x — premium reflects multi-year hyper-growth expectations from AI infrastructure. The multiple is justified as long as hyperscaler capex continues to accelerate. Any capex guidance cut from AWS/Azure/GCP would be the risk event.

analyses.name: Fortinet
analyses.verdict: Elite A+ VCP setup but RSI 71.8 is elevated. TV BUY (13/5) with 5 dissenting sell votes. The best entry is a pullback to the MA50 $124 area — on any dip this becomes a STRONG BUY.

financials.summary: Fortinet delivered a strong recovery year as the SASE (Secure Access Service Edge) and OT (Operational Technology) security markets both accelerated. After a disappointing 2024 where product revenue declined as the company transitioned from hardware to subscription billing, FY2025 showed the subscription model fully inflecting. Annual recurring revenue grew strongly and operating margins expanded as the hardware drag faded. The AI-native security platform refresh drove new enterprise sales cycles.
highlights: Subscription ARR growth accelerated — hardware-to-subscription transition complete
SASE platform gaining enterprise traction — growing vs. Zscaler and Palo Alto Networks
OT (Operational Technology) security: factory cybersecurity is a high-growth vertical
Operating margins expanded on subscription revenue mix shift
Management reinstated growth guidance after the 2024 transition year disappointment

accomplishments: FortiAI platform launched — AI-native security operations integrated across the product suite
OT security wins in manufacturing, energy, and utilities — addressable market expanded
SASE convergence with Lacework acquisition — cloud security adding to platform breadth
FortiGate firewall upgrades driving installed base refresh cycle
Stock recovered above the pre-2024-correction highs as the subscription narrative reset

journey_52w.summary: FTNT spent much of 2024 in repair mode after disappointing guidance reductions. The stock bottomed and began its recovery as subscription revenue metrics inflected. A clean Stage 2 uptrend developed through H1 2025 and has continued. Price $153.62 is significantly above MA50 $124 and MA200 $92. The RSI at 71.8 reflects the pace of the recovery — extended here, but the underlying setup is elite. Patience for a pullback is warranted.

milestones.label: Aug 2025
milestones.note: Q2 2025 earnings — subscription ARR beats consensus; stock breaks out of the 2024 base

milestones.label: Nov 2025
milestones.note: FortiAI platform launch — AI-native security positioned for the new threat landscape

milestones.label: Feb 2026
milestones.note: FY2025 results: hardware-to-subscription transition confirmed complete; guidance restored

milestones.label: Apr 2026
milestones.note: OT security wins in manufacturing verticals disclosed — new addressable market validated

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: VCP 4 contractions forming at $153 level; RSI 71.8 — elevated but setup is elite

risks: RSI 71.8 — extended at current levels; optimal entry is on a pullback
TV: 5 sell votes — meaningful dissent suggesting resistance at current price
Palo Alto Networks and CrowdStrike competition in SASE and XDR is intensifying
Product revenue still below peak levels — full hardware refresh cycle pace uncertain
catalysts: OT cybersecurity mandates from US government (CISA) driving factory security spending
FortiAI adoption — AI-native security detection could be a new revenue driver
Enterprise SASE consolidation deals — multi-product platform wins from point-solution replacements
Q2 2026 earnings — watch for subscription ARR guidance raised again
watch: MA50 $124 pullback = the ideal entry to buy this A+ setup
TV: BUY with 5 sell votes — confirms direction but dissent argues for patience
RSI 71.8 — needs to cool below 65 for the risk/reward to be optimal
critical.valuation: fwd P/E ~35x — premium for a cybersecurity platform but justified by recurring revenue and OT market expansion. Wait for the pullback to the MA50 range for a better entry price.

analyses.name: SpaceX (SPCX)
analyses.verdict: NEW LISTING — SpaceX just listed and is in its first weeks of public trading. Quant signals (52-week breakout, VCP, RS) are not yet meaningful with only days of history. This is a thesis-driven position. The case for SpaceX as a long-term holding is strong; the entry timing is speculative.

financials.summary: SpaceX is the world's leading private launch provider and the operator of Starlink, the largest satellite broadband network in history. Pre-IPO financials are not fully public, but industry estimates suggest revenue in the range of $15-20B+ for FY2025, driven by launch services (Falcon 9, Falcon Heavy, Starship) and Starlink subscriber growth. SpaceX is widely believed to be profitable on an operating basis as Starlink subscribers exceeded 5 million globally. The Starship development program represents a potentially transformational cost reduction in heavy-lift launch.
highlights: Starlink revenue: ~5M+ subscribers at ~$100-120/month = $6-7B annual run rate
Launch business: Falcon 9 manifest fully booked; pricing power evident
Starship development: if successful, cost-per-kg to orbit falls by 10x vs. current vehicles
Pentagon and NASA contract wins confirm government customer base
Starlink broadband entering aviation, maritime, and government enterprise markets

accomplishments: IPO in mid-2026 — one of the most anticipated debuts in years
Starlink reached 5M+ global subscribers — largest satellite broadband network ever built
Starship achieved first successful full orbit test — heavy-lift launch era beginning
FAA launches license for Starship commercial missions approved
SpaceX won multi-billion dollar NASA Artemis moon lander contracts

journey_52w.summary: SPCX has only days of trading history as a public company. The stock is being bought by IPO momentum investors, SpaceX enthusiasts, and institutional managers seeking exposure to the commercial space sector. TV rates it NEUTRAL with only 3 buy and 1 sell vote from limited indicator data — expected for a brand new listing. The quant signals (VCP, RS line, 52-week breakout) require 2-3 months of trading history to become meaningful. This is a fundamental/thesis position, not a technical one.

milestones.label: Mid-2026
milestones.note: IPO — SpaceX lists as a public company; one of the largest tech IPOs in years

milestones.label: Jun 2026
milestones.note: First days of trading — establishing price discovery; institutional allocation underway

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Watch for first post-IPO quarterly update — revenue metrics and Starlink subscriber data

risks: IPO valuation premium — early investors paid up; public market entry price is where value becomes uncertain
Starship technical risk — the entire cost-reduction thesis depends on Starship achieving operational reliability
Regulatory risk: FAA launch licenses can be delayed by environmental review or political pressure
Competition from Blue Origin, United Launch Alliance, and Arianespace in government launch
No historical technical signals yet — pure fundamental bet at this stage
catalysts: First post-IPO earnings / quarterly update — Starlink subscriber growth rate will be the key metric
Starship first commercial launch — proves the reusability economics
Starlink aviation/maritime enterprise contracts — each large contract win is a revenue catalyst
NASA Artemis launch schedule — SpaceX lunar lander deployment timing
watch: TV: NEUTRAL with only 3 buy votes — expected for a brand new listing; not a negative signal
Wait 2-3 months for technical signals to become meaningful before sizing up
Starship launch cadence is the single most important operational metric to follow
critical.valuation: Pre-IPO estimates placed SpaceX at $200-350B valuation — Starlink alone at $5-7B ARR could justify a large portion of that. The Starship optionality (cost reduction enabling new markets like point-to-point cargo, Mars missions) is not easily quantifiable but is real. Hold the position and evaluate after the first quarterly update.
